task_context.actionable_question: Am I a procrastinator and is it costing me marks?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE]. Identify if late submission is systematic. Link to score.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: Your submission patterns indicate a tendency to submit assignments late, which could be impacting your overall engagement and performance. While you are passing your assessments, the average delay suggests a need for improvement in timely submissions.

Insights: Consistent Late Submissions: You have an average submission delay of 3.25 days across your assessments, indicating a systematic pattern of procrastination. This could lead to increased stress and affect your learning experience. | Impact on Scores: Despite submitting late, you have managed to score well on your assessments, with scores ranging from 83 to 100. However, consistent late submissions may hinder your ability to achieve your full potential.

Educational implications: Improving punctuality could enhance your learning experience and reduce stress.

Recommendations: high - Set earlier personal deadlines for assignments. - This can help you manage your time better and reduce the tendency to procrastinate. | medium - Create a study schedule to allocate specific times for completing assessments. - A structured approach can help you stay on track and submit your work on time.

explanation.summary: Your submission patterns indicate a tendency to submit assignments late, which could be impacting your overall engagement and performance. While you are passing your assessments, the average delay suggests a need for improvement in timely submissions.

insights.title: Consistent Late Submissions
insights.description: You have an average submission delay of 3.25 days across your assessments, indicating a systematic pattern of procrastination. This could lead to increased stress and affect your learning experience.

insights.title: Impact on Scores
insights.description: Despite submitting late, you have managed to score well on your assessments, with scores ranging from 83 to 100. However, consistent late submissions may hinder your ability to achieve your full potential.

evidence.context: Scores indicate passing but may not reflect best effort due to delays

educational_implications: Improving punctuality could enhance your learning experience and reduce stress.

recommendations.action: Set earlier personal deadlines for assignments.
recommendations.rationale: This can help you manage your time better and reduce the tendency to procrastinate.

recommendations.action: Create a study schedule to allocate specific times for completing assessments.
recommendations.rationale: A structured approach can help you stay on track and submit your work on time.

confidence.reason: Data is consistent and clearly indicates submission patterns.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether late submission is systematic.
Describe the observed relationship between submission delay and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg as the primary delay metric; do not infer procrastination from score alone.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
